title: Radiated emission fail
problem: A repeatable radiated emission fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: An unintended cable current is acting as an antenna.
The enclosure seam has insufficient shielding.
check_steps: Rotate the turntable and change antenna height to locate the maximum.
Use a current probe to identify the dominant cable path.
corrective_actions: Add common-mode suppression at the cable interface.
Improve seam bonding and repeat the final measurement.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Conducted emission fail
problem: A repeatable conducted emission fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: The switching supply has inadequate input filtering.
A common-mode return path couples noise to the mains.
check_steps: Compare line and neutral spectra with both detector modes.
Probe the input filter and switching node.
corrective_actions: Optimize the common-mode choke and X/Y capacitors.
Shorten the high-current switching loop.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: ESD fail
problem: A repeatable esd fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: The discharge current reaches a sensitive reset line.
Chassis bonding impedance is too high.
check_steps: Reproduce the failing point and polarity.
Monitor reset, power, and clock lines during discharge.
corrective_actions: Add a low-inductance chassis path.
Improve transient protection and firmware recovery.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: EFT fail
title_zh_tw: 快速暫態脈衝群測試失敗
problem: A repeatable eft fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: Burst energy couples through the power input.
I/O filtering is located too far from the connector.
check_steps: Confirm clamp position and coupling length.
Monitor supply rails and communication errors.
corrective_actions: Move filtering to the entry point.
Improve decoupling and cable shielding termination.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Surge fail
problem: A repeatable surge fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: The protection device clamping voltage is too high.
The surge return path has excessive inductance.
check_steps: Verify coupling mode, polarity, and phase angle.
Measure residual voltage at the protected circuit.
corrective_actions: Select a coordinated surge protection network.
Reduce loop area in the surge current path.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Safety hipot fail
problem: A repeatable safety hipot fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: Insulation spacing is insufficient.
Moisture or flux residue lowers insulation resistance.
check_steps: Inspect the failure location before retesting.
Separate components and wiring to isolate the path.
corrective_actions: Correct spacing and insulation construction.
Control cleaning and humidity before the test.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Leakage current fail
problem: A repeatable leakage current fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: Y-capacitance is excessive for the product class.
A wiring error bypasses the intended insulation.
check_steps: Verify the measuring network and supply polarity.
Measure each filter branch separately.
corrective_actions: Rebalance filter capacitance within EMC and safety limits.
Correct wiring and repeat all required conditions.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Ground bond fail
problem: A repeatable ground bond fail result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: A painted surface increases contact resistance.
The protective bonding conductor is undersized.
check_steps: Use a four-wire check across each bond.
Inspect fastener torque and surface preparation.
corrective_actions: Remove insulating coating at the bond point.
Use a compliant conductor and locking hardware.
supervisor_question: How would you confirm the root cause and demonstrate that the correction is effective?
suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Antenna gain mismatch
problem: A repeatable antenna gain mismatch result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: The production antenna differs from the approved sample.
Cable loss was omitted from the gain calculation.

Recalculate gain using calibrated path loss.
corrective_actions: Restore the approved antenna configuration.
Update the technical file when an authorized change applies.

title: Wrong test setup
problem: A repeatable wrong test setup result is found during a controlled compliance evaluation.
background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: The measurement distance is incorrect.
The required auxiliary mode was not activated.
check_steps: Pause the test and compare setup against the method.
Photograph connections and verify instrument settings.
corrective_actions: Rebuild the setup from the approved checklist.
Invalidate affected results and repeat the sequence.

suggested_answer: I would verify the setup, reproduce the result, and change one controlled variable at a time. After identifying the root cause, I would repeat the required test and document objective evidence.

title: Incorrect sample configuration

background: The setup and sample configuration are recorded. The engineer must verify the observation, isolate the coupling or failure path, and preserve traceable evidence.
possible_causes: The wrong firmware or regional mode is installed.
The sample is not transmitting at the required worst case.

Verify mode, channel, bandwidth, and power setting.
corrective_actions: Load the controlled configuration.
Add configuration evidence to the test record.
